description:
<title>Batch-handling log files with find + xargs</title>

    <objective>
        You are acting as a DevOps engineer who has been asked to:
        1. Identify “old” application log files (defined below),
        2. Archive them into a single gzip-compressed tarball,
        3. Remove the originals once they are safely archived,
        4. Analyse the remaining “recent” log files and create an error-statistics
           summary that follows an exact, testable format.
    </objective>

    <background>
        • All work must be done as the normal user in /home/user.  
        • The two applications whose logs you are managing are “app1” and “app2”.  
        • Their logs are already present under  
          /home/user/logs/app1/   and   /home/user/logs/app2/  

          DO NOT create these files yourself; they already exist.  
        • An /home/user/archive/ directory does *not* yet exist and must be created
          by you with default user-writable permissions.  

        Definition of “old” vs “recent” for this exercise:  
        • Consider today’s date to be 2023-09-20 00:00:00.  
        • Any log file whose filename’s date portion is **7 or more days older than
          2023-09-20** (i.e. dated 2023-09-13 or earlier) is “old”.  
        • All others are “recent”.
    </background>

    <requirements>
        1. Use a single gzip-compressed tar archive named  
           /home/user/archive/old-logs.tar.gz  
           containing **only** the “old” log files.  
           a) The paths *inside* the tarball must be stored **relative** to
              /home/user (e.g. “logs/app1/app1-2023-09-01.log”).  
           b) After the archive is created, the original “old” log files must no
              longer exist in their source directories.

        2. Produce a statistics file called  
           /home/user/logs/error_summary_20230920.txt  
           with the following rigid, line-oriented format (exact spelling,
           capitalisation, spacing and newlines will be automatically tested):  

              TOTAL_ERROR_LINES: <integer>
              ERROR_CODE_COUNTS:
              E101 <integer>
              E202 <integer>
              E303 <integer>

           • Count only the lines that remain in the “recent” log files
             after you have removed the old ones.  
           • A line is an error line if it contains the string “ERROR” (case-sensitive).  
           • Extract the 4-character error code between parentheses, e.g.
             “ERROR (E101) …” ⇒ E101.  
           • The file must end with a single trailing newline character.

        3. All searching, archiving, deletion and error-counting should be performed
           using standard UNIX tools; you are specifically expected to employ
           the “find” command combined with “xargs” or “find … -exec …” for
           batch processing rather than iterating file-by-file with shell loops.

        4. Do not print, move or touch any unrelated files or directories.
    </requirements>

    <hints>
        • Start by verifying where the cutoff date (2023-09-13) falls in relation
          to the file names that already exist.  
        • “find … -type f -newermt … ! -newermt …” is one strategy, but using the
          “-mtime” flag is also acceptable; just ensure it selects the correct files
          based on the stated pretend date.  
        • Remember that “tar -czf archive.tar.gz …” expects *paths to add* on
          stdin if you pipe them via xargs.  
        • Use grep (possibly with -o) and sort/uniq or awk to calculate the counts.  
        • Validate the final summary file by manually cat-ing it before you finish.
    </hints>
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth setup BEFORE the agent starts
        ------------------------------------------
    -->
    <initial_state>
        /home/user/logs/app1/app1-2023-09-01.log
            [2023-09-01 12:00:00] INFO  Application started
            [2023-09-01 12:05:10] ERROR (E101) Failed to connect to DB
            [2023-09-01 12:06:00] INFO  Application stopped

        /home/user/logs/app1/app1-2023-09-15.log
            [2023-09-15 09:00:00] INFO  Application started
            [2023-09-15 09:15:27] ERROR (E202) Timeout while processing
            [2023-09-15 10:00:00] ERROR (E101) Failed to connect to DB
            [2023-09-15 11:00:00] INFO  Application stopped

        /home/user/logs/app2/app2-2023-09-10.log
            [2023-09-10 08:00:00] INFO  Start
            [2023-09-10 08:05:00] ERROR (E303) Disk full
            [2023-09-10 08:06:00] INFO  End

        /home/user/logs/app2/app2-2023-09-18.log
            [2023-09-18 07:00:00] INFO  Start
            [2023-09-18 07:30:00] ERROR (E202) Timeout while processing
            [2023-09-18 08:00:00] INFO  End
    </initial_state>

    <!--
        Expected state AFTER the agent finishes
        ---------------------------------------
    -->
    <final_state>
        1. /home/user/archive/ must exist and be user-writable.

        2. Archive:
            Path: /home/user/archive/old-logs.tar.gz
            It must exist and be a valid gzip-compressed tarball whose content list
            (tar -tzf /home/user/archive/old-logs.tar.gz) is exactly:
                logs/app1/app1-2023-09-01.log
                logs/app2/app2-2023-09-10.log
            (Order of the two lines is not important.)

        3. The two “old” log files above must no longer exist in their original
           locations under /home/user/logs/.

        4. The “recent” logs must still be present and unmodified:
               /home/user/logs/app1/app1-2023-09-15.log
               /home/user/logs/app2/app2-2023-09-18.log

        5. Summary file:
            Path: /home/user/logs/error_summary_20230920.txt
            Exact 5-line content (each line terminated by \n):
                TOTAL_ERROR_LINES: 3
                ERROR_CODE_COUNTS:
                E101 1
                E202 2
                E303 0

            The file must have permission 644 (rw-r--r--) or more permissive.

    </final_state>
